findings.summary: All imports satisfied, no missing dependencies, no native modules
Hardcoded SESSION_SECRET fallback; no engines field; no health endpoint — all handled by scaffold
SQLite local file and in-memory sessions are ephemeral on Azure App Service
findings.fix: Migrate to managed database and Redis session store post-deployment

warnings.detail: The app uses express-rate-limit and express-session with secure cookies (secure: production). Azure App Service terminates SSL at the proxy layer. Without trust proxy, req.ip returns the proxy IP so all users share one rate-limit bucket, and the express-session docs explicitly require trust proxy when secure cookies are used behind a proxy.
warnings.fix: Add app.set('trust proxy', 1) at the top of createApp() in src/app.js

warnings.detail: SESSION_SECRET falls back to 'bya-notes-secret-change-in-production' when the env var is not set. The app already warns about this in production but the secret needs to be provisioned as an App Service app setting.
warnings.fix: Set SESSION_SECRET as an App Service application setting (IaC will generate this)

warnings.detail: package.json has no engines field. Oryx will select a Node.js LTS version automatically, which may change between deployments.
warnings.fix: Add engines: { node: '>=20.0.0' } to package.json (scaffold will set WEBSITE_NODE_DEFAULT_VERSION)

warnings.detail: No /health or /healthz endpoint detected. Azure App Service health checks and load balancers use a configurable probe path; without it the root path (/) is used, which serves the full HTML page.
warnings.fix: Scaffold will configure App Service health check path to /

warnings.summary: SQLite local file is ephemeral on Azure App Service
warnings.detail: data.db is written to the app's local filesystem. On App Service, the /home/site/wwwroot filesystem is persistent but instance-local — data is lost on swap, scale-out, or platform repairs unless Azure Files mount is configured.
warnings.fix: Post-deployment: migrate to Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server or Azure SQL

warnings.detail: Sessions are stored in Node.js process memory. Sessions are lost on app restart, scale-out, or slot swap. All users will be logged out on any restart.
warnings.fix: Post-deployment: add Azure Cache for Redis with connect-redis session store

warnings.detail: Repository has no README.md with build/run instructions.
warnings.fix: Add README with build and run instructions

postDeployRecommendations.title: Replace SQLite with a managed database
postDeployRecommendations.reason: SQLite stores data on the ephemeral local filesystem. Data is lost on platform restarts and cannot be shared across scaled-out instances.
services: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server

postDeployRecommendations.title: Replace MemoryStore with Redis session store
postDeployRecommendations.reason: In-process session storage causes all users to be logged out on app restart or when running multiple instances.

postDeployRecommendations.title: Add README with build and run instructions
postDeployRecommendations.reason: No README detected. Documentation helps contributors and operators understand how to run and deploy the app.
